page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.2xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an ARM64 architecture utilizing an 8-core AWS Graviton processor based on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 design. The instance provides 8 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz, with 1 thread per core, and is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and EBS optimization, while delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded compression and decompression tasks placing in the top 25%. Passmark memory benchmarks rank in the top 10%, reflecting high-efficiency memory access, though Passmark CPU encryption tests show weaker relative performance. Multi-core operations, including stress-ng multi-core tests, software compilation with Clang, and Redis SET operations, consistently perform in the average range. For machine learning workloads, the instance handles small-scale LLM inference with average throughput but exhibits poor prompt processing speeds on medium-scale models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture and dedicated core allocation offer a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads without the premium of specialized accelerator hardware. The high network bandwidth and strong single-threaded performance make this instance suitable for application servers, static web serving, text processing, and database operations that benefit from high memory throughput and a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.2xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and powered by an ARM64 AWS Graviton processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores based on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong single-core performance in text processing and single-threaded compression, alongside top-tier memory performance in Passmark testing. Conversely, CPU encryption benchmarks are weak, and medium-scale LLM inference is limited. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring balanced compute and memory resources, making it well-suited for web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Graviton physical cores, 32 GB of RAM, and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, optimized for balanced workloads.

tagline: A balanced ARM64 virtual server featuring dedicated Graviton cores, high-speed memory throughput, and optimized network performance for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory with top-tier bandwidth
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and EBS optimization
Strong single-threaded compression and text processing performance
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and database tasks
